payload.title: Utz bootstrap exposed seven remaining client Site ergonomics gaps
payload.summary: The Utz client-service Site setup was much cleaner than Staccato, but it still exposed seven ergonomics gaps that should be addressed in Narada's Site bootstrap and validation machinery.
evidence: During Utz setup, the operator asked whether new ergonomics issues remained. The Site was created successfully at .narada, with config validation and canonical inbox file-drop dry-run passing. The remaining friction was not conceptual confusion but missing first-class operations around bootstrap, validation, path rendering, registry update, evidence, and durability posture.
proposal: Address these seven gaps: 1. Add a governed client Site bootstrap command that inspects workspace, proposes shape, creates .narada, installs canonical inbox, writes guidance, and validates. 2. Add a site doctor command that checks config, required folders, inbox file-drop, runtime DB posture, routing refs, durable/volatile path policy, and Site-local assumptions. 3. Add host-aware path rendering so Windows paths and WSL command forms are generated rather than hand-written. 4. Clarify and automate empty-folder tracking so .gitkeep mechanics do not leak into operator reasoning. 5. Update Site registry/addressing automatically or produce an explicit registration outbox/inbox item when a new Site is created. 6. Represent bootstrap as intent -> dry-run plan -> approval -> execute -> evidence rather than direct mutation followed by retrospective observation. 7. Define and validate OneDrive-backed non-Git Site posture separately from Git-backed Sites.
payload.recommendation: Treat these as follow-up implementation candidates for the client Site bootstrap arc. The highest leverage first step is a site bootstrap dry-run/execution command paired with site doctor, because those two surfaces can absorb most of the repeated manual validation and boilerplate.
